english: haste, hurry, *tsa-dág jhe śig* (*tsha drag byed shig*) C., *tsa-rág toṅ* (*tsha rag tong*) W. make haste! -- adv. tsa-drág-tu (tsha drag tu) Sch. but also *mā tsa-rág śog* (*ma tsha rag shog*) W. come quickly, without delay!

anxiety about, tender care for a thing, ni. f.; *tsa-na-čan* (*tsha na can*) W. solicitous, careful, attached, *tsa-na-méd-ḱan* (*tsha na med mkhan*) W. indifferent, unfeeling, callous; ḱáṅ-pe *tsá-na kúr-ḱan* (*khang pa'i tsha na khur mkhan*) W. one that has to care for the welfare of a household or community, superintendent etc.

I. vb. to be hot, só-ga-(la) nyi-mai ˛od-zér ráb-(tu-) tsá-bas (so ga (la) nyi ma'i 'od zer rab (tu) tsha bas) as at the time of the Soga the rays of the sun are very hot. II. sbst. 1. heat, tsá-bas ydúṅ-ba (tsha bas gdung ba) to be tormented by the heat S.g.; tsá-bai dus-su (tsha ba'i dus su) during the heat of the day, at noon, cf. dro (dro) Mil.; tsá-ba ni bsíl-bar gyúr-to (tsha ba ni bsil bar gyur to) the heat changed into coolness Dzl.; tsa ɣzér-ba (tsha gzer ba) the burning of the heat, or of the sun Sch.; tsá-bai nad (tsha ba'i nad) Lt. the fever-stage in diseases; tsa sél-ba (tsha sel ba) to cure an acute disease Sch.; tsá-bas rmyá-ba (tsha bas rmya ba) to lose one's appetite in consequence of great heat Sch. -- 2. warm food, stér-ba (ster ba), ˛drén-pa ('dren pa) Mil; tsa-ɣčíg-ma (tsha gcig ma) one that in twenty-four hours takes but one regular meal. -- 3. spice, condiment, tsá-ba ɣsum (tsha ba gsum) त्रिकटुक, black pepper, long pepper, ginger. III. adj. (vulgo *tsém-mo* (*tshem mo*) C., *tsán-te* (*tshan te*) W.) 1. hot, warm. -- 2. sharp, biting, pungent, of spices etc. -- 3. stinging, prickly, thorny Pth. -- Comp. tsa-˛kru (tsha 'khru) colic, gripes Lt. -- tsa-góṅ (tsha gong) forenoon Sch. -- tsa-graṅ (tsha grang) 1. hot and cold. 2. (relative) warmth. -- *tsan-gyal* (*tshan gyal*) W. inflammatory fever. -- tsa-lčíb (tsha lcib) v. lčíb (lcib). -- *tsa-čú* (*tsha chu*) 1. a hot spring C. 2. a warm bath C. -- tsa-bra (tsha bra) dinner Sch.(?) -- tsa-mig (tsha mig) red pepper Ld. -- *tsém-mo* (*tshem mo*) C. hot, warm. -- tsa-dmyál (tsha dmyal) hot hell. -- tsa-zér (tsha zer) 'glowing ray', po. for sun. -- *tsan-láṅ* (*tshan lang*) W. hot, passionate, ardent; in the rut. -- tsa-lam (tsha lam) Sch.: half a day's journey, a march before breakfast, = tsal-mai lam (tshal ma'i lam). -- tsa-bsubs (tsha bsubs) Lt.?

resp. dbón-po (dbon po) B., sku-tsa (sku tsha) C. 1. grandchild, grandson, Ld. *mē-mē-tsa-wo* (*me me tsha bo*). -- 2. nephew, brother's son Dzl.; Ld.: *à-żàṅ-tsa-wo* (*a zhang tsha bo*). -- bú-tsa (bu tsha) v. bu (bu); yáṅ-tsa (yang tsha) great-grandchild, yúṅ-tsa (yung tsha) great-great-grandchild, ɣzì-tsa (gzi tsha) id. Sch. -- tsa-skór (tsha skor) grandchildren Sch. -- tsá-mo (tsha mo) 1. granddaughter. 2. niece. 3. wife Lh. -- tsa-żáṅ (tsha zhang) nephew and uncle Mil. -- tsa-ɣzúg (tsha gzug) nephews and nieces Sch. -- tsa-yúg (tsha yug) grandchildren, tsa-yúg máṅ-poi čó-lo (tsha yug mang po'i co lo) the many grandchildren's tattling Mil.; offspring, in gen., bu-tsa-yúg (bu tsha yug) id. W., C.; *tsá-wo tsa-yúg yáṅ-tsa yúṅ-tsa* (*tsha bo tsha yug yang tsha yung tsha*) W. children and children's children.
1. v. tsá-ba (tsha ba). -- 2. v. tsá-bo (tsha bo).

1. little images of Buddha, and conical figures, moulded of clay and used at sacrifices Schl. 194, 206; tsá-kaṅ (tsha khang) place for keeping them Cs.; fig. ḱá-nas mé-yi tsá-tsa ˛pro (kha nas me yi tsha tsha 'phro) from his mouth proceeded cones of fire Pth. -- 2. Bal. for tsa-drág (tsha drag) hastily, quickly; tsa-tsa-méd (tsha tsha med) slow, slowly.
lamb-skin, *tsar-lág* (*tshar lag*) W. coat made of lamb-skins.
a kind of medicine Med., acc. to Wdṅ. = dar-tsúr (dar tsur).
1. also mtsa-lú (mtsha lu) (?) cock, bya(-po)-tsá-lu (bya (po) tsha lu) Wdṅ., C.; in W. applied only to red-breasted cocks, from mtsal (mtshal) vermilion (Sch. hen?). -- 2. v. tsál-ba (tshal ba).
C. sweet orange, frq. in Sik.
1. Ssk. सुभग, Hd. सुहागा, Pers. تنکار, Ar. بورق, borax, tsá-lei skyúr- (tsha le'i skyur -)
